items.question: When should you use logging instead of print?
items.answer: Use logging for any application or library: it adds severity levels, named loggers, and pluggable destinations/formats you can control centrally. print is acceptable only for throwaway scripts or intentional program output.

items.question: Why does logging.info(...) sometimes produce no output?
items.answer: Because the effective level defaults to WARNING. INFO and DEBUG are filtered out until you lower the threshold (e.g. logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)).

items.question: What breaks if a library calls logging.basicConfig() itself?
items.answer: It can attach handlers/levels onto the root logger for every consumer, causing duplicate or forced output the application didn't ask for. Libraries should only getLogger and emit.
